mission.title: parsec: Memory cleanup of sensitive data
mission.description: Memory cleanup of sensitive data. Community-reported issue.

steps.title: Identify parsec setup
steps.description:
Check your Parsec service installation:
```bash
parsec-tool ping
```
Parsec is a Platform AbstRaction for SECurity service. This issue is about ensuring sensitive data (keys, secrets) is properly zeroed from memory after use.

steps.title: Review the security concern
steps.description:
From the PSA Crypto API version 1.0.0, section 6.3.3:

> Implementations must wipe all sensitive data from memory when it is no longer used. It is recommended that they wipe this sensitive data as soon as possible.

The Parsec service handles cryptographic keys and secrets. Memory cleanup of sensitive data prevents data leakage through memory reuse.

steps.title: Apply the fix for memory cleanup of sensitive data
steps.description:
The fix involves using the `zeroize` crate to wipe sensitive values from memory when they are no longer needed. Key changes:
1. Add the `zeroize` crate as a dependency
2. Implement `Zeroize` for types that hold sensitive data (keys, secrets)
3. Use `Zeroizing<T>` wrappers or explicit `zeroize()` calls when values go out of scope

See the [source issue](https://github.com/parallaxsecond/parsec/issues/122) for community-verified solutions and implementation details.

steps.title: Verify the fix works
steps.description:
Confirm that Parsec still functions correctly after the memory cleanup changes:
```bash
parsec-tool ping
parsec-tool list-keys
```
Verify that cryptographic operations complete successfully and that sensitive data is no longer retained in memory after use.

resolution.summary: Resolved by integrating the zeroize crate to ensure sensitive cryptographic data (keys, secrets) is wiped from memory as soon as it is no longer needed, following PSA Crypto API section 6.3.3 guidelines.

prerequisites.description: A working Parsec installation or development environment with Rust toolchain.
